Mtn For Judgmnt On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Feb-14-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. ELIZABETH A. MCGINTY OF ANWEYL, SCOFFIED & STEPP LLP, TEL. NO.
(916) 565-1800 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT FREE AXEZ USA LLC BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. ARI A. BARUTH OF LONG & LEVIT LLP, TEL. NO. 438-4596 APPEARING FOR THE CROSS-DEFENDANT RUDOLPH AND SLETTEN, INC.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS DEFENDANTS CEITRONICS, INC. and CUPERTINO ELECTRIC, INC.'s Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is Granted with ten days leave to amend as to the first cause of action for equitable indemnity. Granted without leave to amend as to the second and sixth causes of action for contribution and declaratory relief. In order to be liable for equitable indemnity, there must be potential for joint and several liability in the underlying action. (BFGC Architects Planners, Inc. v. Forcum/Mackey Construction, Inc. (2004) 119 Cal.App.4th 848, 853, "The doctrine applies only among defendants who are jointly and severally liable to the plaintiff.") In other words, had the plaintiff in the underlying action sued the party against whom equitable indemnity is sought against, the plaintiff would have the potential to recover against that party in tort. The First Amended Cross-Complaint ("FACC") does not plead facts establishing that Cross-Defendant could be held jointly and severally liable for intentional or negligent misrepresentation, the torts alleged in the underlying complaint. No facts are alleged that Cross-Defendant made any representation to the plaintiff in the underlying case. Additionally, assuming breach of warranty is a tort cause of action, the FACC does not plead facts establishing that Cross-Defendant could be held liable for the breach of implied warranty of merchantability or fitness. The FACC does not plead that Cross-Defendants sold any goods to the underlying plaintiff. (See Fogo v. Cutter Laboratories, Inc. (1977) 68 Cal.App.3d 744, 758, "'a sale is ordinarily an essential element of any warranty, express or implied.") Cross-Plaintiff is given leave to amend to allege facts, if it can in good faith, establishing that Cross-Defendant could be held jointly and severally liable in tort in the underlying action. As to the second cause of action, the FACC does not plead that a money judgment has been jointly rendered against Cross-Plaintiff and Cross-Defendant. (See CCP sec. 875.) As to the sixth cause of action, the only controversy alleged between the parties is whether Cross-Plaintiff is entitled to equitable indemnity, and therefore, this cause of action is duplicative. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/EHG)
